ingredients.item:
This Vietnamese version of sauerkraut goes perfectly with braised or fried dishes. When served as a side dish for a weeknight family meal, bắp cải muối is often accompanied by a bowl of vegan fish dipping sauce. Rich in probiotics, vitamins, minerals, and fiber, this pickle helps maintain healthy gut flora and supports the immune system.
SKILL LEVELModerate|YIELD2 (47-ounce/1.4-L) glass jars

BRINE
6 cups (1.5 L) water
1½ tablespoons salt

1 tablespoon sugar
½ cup (120 ml) rice rinse water
ingredients.original:
This Vietnamese version of sauerkraut goes perfectly with braised or fried dishes. When served as a side dish for a weeknight family meal, bắp cải muối is often accompanied by a bowl of vegan fish dipping sauce. Rich in probiotics, vitamins, minerals, and fiber, this pickle helps maintain healthy gut flora and supports the immune system.
SKILL LEVELModerate|YIELD2 (47-ounce/1.4-L) glass jars

BRINE
6 cups (1.5 L) water
1½ tablespoons salt

1 tablespoon sugar
½ cup (120 ml) rice rinse water

ingredients.item:
VEGETABLES
7 ounces (200 g) carrots, peeled and julienned (see Note)
1 small head white cabbage (about 1¾ pounds/800 g), thinly sliced
ingredients.original:
VEGETABLES
7 ounces (200 g) carrots, peeled and julienned (see Note)
1 small head white cabbage (about 1¾ pounds/800 g), thinly sliced

ingredients.item: 3 ounces (90 g) roughly chopped Vietnamese mint leaves
ingredients.original: 3 ounces (90 g) roughly chopped Vietnamese mint leaves

ingredients.item: 1. MAKE THE BRINE In a large saucepan, combine all ingredients except the rice water, and bring to a boil over high heat. Once salt and sugar are dissolved, remove from heat and let cool.
ingredients.original: 1. MAKE THE BRINE In a large saucepan, combine all ingredients except the rice water, and bring to a boil over high heat. Once salt and sugar are dissolved, remove from heat and let cool.

ingredients.item: 2. Stir in the rice water and then pour into jars with the vegetables you want to pickle.
ingredients.original: 2. Stir in the rice water and then pour into jars with the vegetables you want to pickle.

ingredients.item: 3. PREP AND PACK THE VEGETABLES In a large bowl, combine all ingredients and mix well.
ingredients.original: 3. PREP AND PACK THE VEGETABLES In a large bowl, combine all ingredients and mix well.

ingredients.item: 4. Place carrots, cabbage, and mint leaves in two 47-ounce (1.4 L) sterilized glass jars (see here) and cover with brine. Use two bamboo skewers to press the vegetables into the brine until completely submerged.
ingredients.original: 4. Place carrots, cabbage, and mint leaves in two 47-ounce (1.4 L) sterilized glass jars (see here) and cover with brine. Use two bamboo skewers to press the vegetables into the brine until completely submerged.

ingredients.item: 5. Close the lid tightly and leave jar undisturbed in a cool place for 2 to 3 days.
ingredients.original: 5. Close the lid tightly and leave jar undisturbed in a cool place for 2 to 3 days.

ingredients.item: 6. When pickled and sour enough to your liking, store in the refrigerator for up to 1 month.
ingredients.original: 6. When pickled and sour enough to your liking, store in the refrigerator for up to 1 month.
